# ML training test fixtures

## `fixture_ohlcv.csv`

60 synthetic OHLCV bars — deterministic sine + linear drift. Generated by
`make_fixture.py`. Enough warmup for all 30+ features (longest lookback = EMA-200
falls back gracefully, ADX-14 needs 15 bars, BB-20 needs 20 bars).

Columns: `open_time,open,high,low,close,volume,taker_buy_volume`.
Times start at `1700000000` and step 3600 s (1h interval).

## `fixture_expected.json`

Ground-truth feature vector for the LAST bar of `fixture_ohlcv.csv`, computed by
Python `feature_engine.build_features`. One object with `FEATURE_NAMES` as keys
and numeric values to ~16 sig figs.

Regenerate with:

```bash
cd elibri-backend/ml-training
python make_fixture.py          # rewrites CSV
python emit_expected.py         # reruns feature_engine, rewrites JSON
```

## Why this matters

The Go side (`internal/ml/features_v2.go:ExtractFeaturesV2`) MUST compute an
identical vector for the same input. Any drift between the two implementations
has, historically, silently mistrained thresholds (Patch 2G's adx/100 bug).
The parity test at `internal/ml/features_v2_parity_test.go` loads both artifacts
and asserts `|go - python| <= 1e-9` element-wise. Update this fixture ONLY when
you intentionally change a feature formula on both sides simultaneously.
